# convert a polar scan into a spatial object.

Georeferences the center of pixels for a scan into a
SpatialPointsDataFrame object.

## Usage

``` r
scan_to_spatial(scan, lat, lon, k = 4/3, re = 6378, rp = 6357)
```

## Arguments

- scan:

  a scan (sweep) of class scan

- lat:

  Geodetic latitude of the radar in degrees. If missing taken from
  `scan`.

- lon:

  Geodetic longitude of the radar in degrees. If missing taken from
  `scan`.

- k:

  Numeric. Standard refraction coefficient.

- re:

  Numeric. Earth equatorial radius, in km.

- rp:

  Numeric. Earth polar radius, in km.

## Value

a SpatialPointsDataFrame

## Details

Beam altitude accounts for the curvature of the earth, using
[beam_height](http://adriaandokter.com/bioRad/reference/beam_height.md).
Distance from the radar over the earth's surface is calculated using
[beam_distance](http://adriaandokter.com/bioRad/reference/beam_distance.md).

## Examples

``` r
# load example scan:
data(example_scan)

# convert to a SpatialPointsDataFrame:
scan_to_spatial(example_scan)
```
